_patch_meta.description: VCB-030: H7965K (mti=2511, 8v), H7965J (mti=2510, 5v), H7965H (mti=406, 1v), H7965L (mti=2512, 1v). All FRESH. H7965L all-verses-fail (adverbial use, no inner-being). Shalom reinstated 2026-05-01.

record.context_description: Term names shalom as the substantive peace-blessing invoked in greeting — the comprehensive peace of the whole person called upon as the content of the greeting act

operations.description: New group for H7965K: shalom as substantive peace-blessing in greeting

record.notes: Peace be to you, and peace be to your house, and peace be to all that you have — the triple sha.lom blessing as greeting content; sha.lom is the substantive blessing invoked, not merely a social formula.

operations.description: 1Sa 25:6 — ANCHOR, group 2511-001: triple sha.lom blessing as greeting content

record.context_description: Term names shalom as the inner relational bond of close friendship — the bond of trust, loyalty, and mutual allegiance whose violation by the sha.lom-friend constitutes the deepest relational betrayal

operations.description: New group for H7965J: shalom as inner bond of close friendship

record.notes: If you have come to me in friendship (sha.lom) to help me, my heart will be joined to you — sha.lom as inner relational loyalty directly connected to heart-union.

operations.description: 1Ch 12:17 — ANCHOR, group 2510-001: sha.lom friendship connected to heart-union

record.notes: My close friend (ish sha.lom) in whom I trusted — sha.lom names the bond of friendship whose betrayal is devastating.

operations.description: Psa 41:9 — related, group 2510-001: man of sha.lom who betrays

record.notes: Stretched out his hand against his friends (sha.lom); he violated his covenant — sha.lom friendship violated alongside covenant.

operations.description: Psa 55:20 — related, group 2510-001: sha.lom friends; covenant violation

record.notes: All my close friends (anshei sha.lom), watching for my fall — men of sha.lom who are secretly hostile.

operations.description: Jer 20:10 — related, group 2510-001: men of sha.lom secretly hostile

record.notes: Your trusted friends (anshei sha.lom) have deceived you — men of sha.lom who deceive.

operations.description: Jer 38:22 — related, group 2510-001: men of sha.lom who deceive

record.context_description: Term names shalom as the divine identity commemorated in the altar name YHWH-Shalom — God identified as peace in the altar built after the peace-theophany, sealing the inner peace given in divine encounter

operations.description: New group for H7965H: shalom as divine identity in YHWH-Shalom altar name

record.notes: Gideon built an altar and called it YHWH-Shalom (The Lord Is Peace) following the theophany of Judg 6:23. The divine name commemorates God as the source and substance of peace sealed in the altar after the inner-peace encounter (Peace be to you. Do not fear).

operations.description: Judg 6:24 — ANCHOR, group 406-001: YHWH-Shalom altar name — God identified as peace

record.notes: Sha.lom used adverbially meaning 'completely/wholly' applied to the external event of exile. Adverbial intensifier; inner-being content of exile carried by other terms, not by sha.lom in this adverbial role.

operations.description: Jer 13:19 — set aside: sha.lom as adverb 'wholly', external event, no inner-being

all_verses_fail_terms: H7965L (mti=2512) — 1 verse, sha.lom adverbial, no inner-being. R4 not applicable.
